HUGH Bigod, son of ROGER Bigod Earl of Norfolk & his wife Ida --- (-[11/18] Feb 1225).  “Rogerus …

memoryArtifacts.headline: Arms of Bigod - pre-1269
memoryArtifacts.description:
The surname Bigod was first found in Essex at Dunmow and Finchingfield, where they were granted lands by King William after the Norman Conquest in 1066. Roger Bigod is also listed in the Doomsday Book; he was a Sheriff, with large land holdings in Essex, Norfolk and Suffolk.

Before the advent of the printing press and the first dictionaries, the English language was not standardized. Sound was what guided spelling in the Middle Ages, so one person's name was often recorded under several variations during a single lifetime. Spelling variations were common, even among the names of the most literate people. Known variations of the Bigod family name include Bigot, Bigode, Bygod, Begod, Bigod, Wigot, Bidgood and many more.

popularStory.headline: Career
popularStory.description: In 1215 he was one of the twenty-five sureties of the Magna Carta of King John. He succeeded to his father's estates including Framlingham Castle in 1221.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk於1186年2月18日出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可特福德時，他的父親Roger Bigod 3rd Earl of Norfolk and Suffolk42歲，母親Ida de Tosny Countess of Norfolk25歲。他於大約1206年與Maud Matilda Marshall結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世於1225年2月18日在英國諾福爾可特福德享年39歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾福爾可特福德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk于1186年2月18日出生在英国英格兰諾福爾可特福德时，他的父亲Roger Bigod 3rd Earl of Norfolk and Suffolk42岁，母亲Ida de Tosny Countess of Norfolk25岁。他于大约1206年与Maud Matilda Marshall结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他去世于1225年2月18日在英国諾福爾可特福德享年39岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾福爾可特福德。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Bigod,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Jaroslaw, Jozef, Krystyna, Slawomir.

Polish and Jewish (from Poland): from bigos, formerlydenoting anything that was chopped or slashed, now a particular dishcomposed of hash and sauerkraut; also ‘confusion’; hence perhaps anickname for someone who caused confusion.

nameMeanings.name: Earl
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English e(o)rl ‘earl’, a traditional English title of nobility equivalent in rank to French and Norman Count .

variants.name: Earle
meanings: English: variant of   Earl  .

variants.name: Harle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Dieter, Johann, Kurt.
South German (Härle): nickname from a diminutive of Middle High German hār ‘hair’.

English (northern) and Scottish: habitational name from Kirkharle and Little Harle in Northumberland (earlier simply Herle, Harle), possibly named from an Old English personal name Herela (a derivative of various compound names with the first element here ‘army’) + Old English lēah ‘wood, clearing’.

variants.name: Earls
meanings: English: variant of Earl with genitival -s, denoting someone who was a member of an earl's household, or, with post-medieval excrescent -s. In Ireland, when this is not the English name, it is from Gaelic Mac an Iarla ‘son of the earl’.

variants.name: Hearl
meanings: English: variant of Earl with prosthetic H-.

variants.name: Count
meanings: English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French counte ‘count’ (Old French conte, from Latin comes, comitem); a title of French and Norman nobility, ranking as equivalent to English Earl .

variants.name: Earll
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Earl  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Norfolk
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from the county of Norfolk in East Anglia, so called from an Old English tribal name composed of the elements north ‘north’ + folc ‘people’ (in contrast to the sūthfolc of Suffolk).

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk was born on 18 February 1186, in Thetford, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, Roger Bigod 3rd Earl of Norfolk and Suffolk, was 42 and his mother, Ida de Tosny Countess of Norfolk, was 25. He married Maud Matilda Marshall about 1206. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 6 daughters. He died on 18 February 1225, in Thetford, Norfolk, England, at the age of 39, and was buried in Thetford, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk est né le 18 février 1186, à Thetford, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Roger Bigod 3rd Earl of Norfolk and Suffolk, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Ida de Tosny Countess of Norfolk, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Maud Matilda Marshall environ 1206. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 6 filles. Il est décédé le 18 février 1225, à Thetford, Norfolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 39 ans, et a été enterré à Thetford, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.Thetford St Mary the Less, Suffolk, Engl: Thetford St Mary the Less, Suffolk, England
de.lifeSummary: Als Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk am 18. Februar 1186 in Thetford, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Roger Bigod 3rd Earl of Norfolk and Suffolk 42 und seine Mutter Ida de Tosny Countess of Norfolk 25. Er hat um 1206 Maud Matilda Marshall geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Er starb am 18. Februar 1225 im Alter von 39 Jahren in Thetford, Norfolk, England und wurde in Thetford, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom begraben.

Lui sposato Maud Matilda Marshall circa 1206. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 18 febbraio 1225, nel Thetford, Norfolk, England, all'età di 39 anni, e fu sepolto a Thetford, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ko.lifeSummary: Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk(이)가 1186년 2월 18일 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 테트퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Roger Bigod 3rd Earl of Norfolk and Suffolk(은)는 42세였고, 그의 어머니 Ida de Tosny Countess of Norfolk(은)는 25세였다. 그는 대략 1206년 Maud Matilda Marshall(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1225년 2월 18일 영국 노르폴크 테트퍼드에서 39세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 테트퍼드에 묻혔다.

pt.Thetford St Mary the Less, Suffolk, Engl: Thetford St Mary the Less, Suffolk, Inglaterra
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk nasceu em 18 de fevereiro de 1186, em Thetford, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Roger Bigod 3rd Earl of Norfolk and Suffolk, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Ida de Tosny Countess of Norfolk, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Maud Matilda Marshall aproximadamente 1206. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 6 filhas. Ele faleceu em 18 de fevereiro de 1225, em Thetford, Norfolk, Inglaterra, com 39 anos, e foi sepultado em Thetford, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Thetford St Mary the Less, Suffolk, Engl: тетфорд ст мэри т лесс, суффолк, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk родился 18 февраля 1186 г. в местности тетфорд, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Roger Bigod 3rd Earl of Norfolk and Suffolk было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Ida de Tosny Countess of Norfolk, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Maud Matilda Marshall около 1206 г. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Он умер 18 февраля 1225 г. в местности тетфорд, норфолк, Англия в возрасте 39 лет и был похоронен в местности тетфорд, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания.

es.18 February 1186–18 February 1225: 18 de febrero de 1186–18 de febrero de 1225
es.Thetford St Mary the Less, Suffolk, Engl: Thetford St Mary the Less, Suffolk, Inglaterra
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk nació el 18 de febrero de 1186, en Thetford, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Roger Bigod 3rd Earl of Norfolk and Suffolk, tenía 42 años y su mamá, Ida de Tosny Countess of Norfolk, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Maud Matilda Marshall aproximadamente 1206. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 18 de febrero de 1225, en Thetford, Norfolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 39 años, y fue sepultado en Thetford, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
